# Substrate-restart recovery (two-mode: idle-out + sunset)

## Context

@tobiu directed an architectural pivot 2026-05-04 (session `cce1fea5-32ff-410c-b820-2e9a27b3cd51`) after the post-merge runaway-spawn pattern of Cmd+N-based recovery (Phase 1 of #10601, shipped via #10619 / #10620 / #10621) became apparent. The prompt-layer fix proposed in #10627 (and partially implemented in PR #10670, now abandoned) treats a symptom of the deeper substrate bug rather than the root cause.

Cross-family substrate-truth audit refined the design space; this Epic captures the consensus shape from @neo-gpt + @neo-gemini-3-1-pro + @neo-opus-4-7 in same-session A2A coordination.

## The Problem

**Empirical anchor 1: runaway spawn pattern.** `heartbeat-opus_4_7.log` shows 9 actual `Successfully resumed @neo-opus-4-7 via osascript (Claude)` between 14:41-16:02 CEST (2026-05-03), with "Last memory is N min old" reason growing 31m → 242m. None of the spawned sessions produced a fresh `AGENT_MEMORY` because @tobiu manually ESC-rejected each one — the substrate had no signal channel for that rejection.

**Empirical anchor 2: multi-day accumulation.** Claude Desktop's "Recents" panel shows 19+ orphan boot-titled sessions across 5+ prompt-iteration generations.

**Empirical anchor 3: MC-server staleness confirmed.** `set_session_id` boot call this session returned `replacedSessionId: f839713d-...` — yesterday's Row 2 session. `SessionService.currentSessionId` survives across MCP-client reconnects when the server stays running.

**Root cause:** parallel-session identity-uniqueness violation during long boot windows. Boot ramp (read AGENTS_STARTUP.md + AGENTS.md + MEMORY.md + tools) takes longer than the 5-min heartbeat interval. Even WITHOUT user rejection, slow boot triggers a parallel spawn before the in-progress one saves its first memory. Result: parallel `@neo-opus-4-7` sessions racing on:

- A2A targeting (Gemini sends to one identity, multiple sessions race to read+`mark_read`)
- Inbox `mark_read` collisions (siblings blind to messages already-read)
- Memory Core sessionId collisions
- Sunset semantics (drops one `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION`; N-1 zombie sessions remain subscribed)
- Dream/REM synthesis (parallel narratives = synthesis garbage)

## The Architectural Reality

The recovery substrate today (Phase 1 of #10601) lives in:

- `ai/scripts/swarm-heartbeat.sh` — cron-driven 5-min poll
- `ai/scripts/checkSunsetted.mjs` — predicate; emits `sunsetted: true` only on missing `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` per #10641 fix
- `ai/scripts/resumeHarness.mjs:120-192` — fires Cmd+N + osascript paste of boot-grounding prompt
- `ai/scripts/wakeSafetyGate.mjs` — fail-closed safety primitive (#10648); currently `tripped` since 2026-05-03 22:53Z

Root substrate bugs in the Cmd+N approach:
1. Cmd+N inside running Claude Desktop process keeps the SAME MCP client connection alive — `SessionService.currentSessionId` doesn't reset
2. The 600s cooldown is time-based, not in-flight-aware — boot ramp easily exceeds 600s
3. OS process boundary doesn't enforce identity uniqueness AT THE DATA LAYER (multiple instances can race on shared SQLite + mailbox)

## The Fix

**Two-mode recovery, gated by `checkSunsetted.mjs`:**

**Idle-out mode (active subscription + stale memory):**
- Send A2A heartbeat message INTO existing session via `add_message({to: identity, body: ...})`
- `bridge-daemon.mjs` existing in-place keystroke logic delivers — zero new transport
- Subscription/sessionId/MC continuity preserved
- Bounded, non-spawning, idempotent, no-destructive-type guarantees (carries forward #10641's lesson — staleness as lower-authority signal, NOT "agent is idle")
- In-flight cooldown marker — prevents A2A spam during active long-turn / rate-limit

**Sunset mode (no subscription):**
- Per-harness terminal-restart of harness process — establishes fresh OS process + fresh MCP client handshake + fresh `currentSessionId` by construction
- **Restart must be proven per harness** — `open -a` typically activates an existing process, NOT spawns fresh; investigation tracks must discover the actual restart primitive (quit+open / `open -na` / harness CLI / app-server API)
- Boot prompt simply: read `AGENTS_STARTUP.md`, then `add_memory` once as boot heartbeat, then proceed normally — NO `set_session_id` plumbing
- `originSessionId` retained as non-authoritative retrieval hint for SUNSET-tagged memory lookup, NOT as `set_session_id` argument

**Identity-uniqueness mutex (in-flight boot/restart lock — covers BOTH modes):**
- `resumeHarness.mjs` writes `inflight-{mode}-{identity}.txt` with timestamp + lock-id before action
- Next heartbeat fire skips action if lock exists AND no fresh `AGENT_MEMORY` since lock timestamp
- Spawned/nudged agent's first `add_memory` clears the lock implicitly (or explicitly via `checkSunsetted`)
- Lock age > `BOOT_TIMEOUT` (~15 min) without fresh memory = abandoned action — catches user-ESC + genuine-failure + MC-staleness + rate-limit identically; auto-trips wake safety gate after N consecutive abandoned actions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** `checkSunsetted.mjs` evolves to emit BOTH `sunset` AND `idle_out_candidate` signals with explicit evidence fields (not implicit boolean)
- [ ] **(AC2)** `resumeHarness.mjs` forks into two paths: idle-out → A2A `add_message`; sunset → terminal-restart with prompt injection
- [ ] **(AC3)** In-flight lock primitive guards both paths; first `add_memory` post-action clears it
- [ ] **(AC4)** Wake safety gate auto-trips after N consecutive abandoned in-flight locks for any single identity
- [ ] **(AC5)** Per-harness terminal-restart investigation produces empirical proof for each: old process gone OR MCP transport restarted; first `healthcheck`/`add_memory` proves fresh `currentSessionId`; no duplicate spawned (Claude Desktop, Antigravity, Codex Desktop)
- [ ] **(AC6)** Idle-out A2A nudge body convention documented + cooldown semantics enforced; no destructive-type-into-active-draft
- [ ] **(AC7)** #10627 close-as-superseded; PR #10670 closed unmerged; `set_session_id` plumbing in boot prompt removed from `resumeHarness.mjs:47-56`
- [ ] **(AC8)** Incident forensic record captures the runaway-spawn pattern: timeline, log evidence, parallel-session identity-uniqueness violation pathway, ESC-as-rejection failure mode

## Out of Scope

- Stage 2 polish: harness CLIs (Claude CLI, Codex CLI) for cleaner restart UX — flagged but not blocking; nice-to-have once restart primitive is empirically proven per harness
- Re-enabling `autoDream` / `autoGoldenPath` — governed by #10569 hard-stop
- MC server lifecycle restructuring — governed by #10186

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: `open -a Claude` guarantees fresh MCP.** Empirically false — typically activates existing process. Per-harness investigation must discover real restart primitive; AC5 enforces empirical proof.
- **Trap: prompt-layer fix for substrate bug.** #10627 attempted `set_session_id` rotation in boot prompt; this Epic supersedes that approach because process-boundary trap (subprocess singleton mutation ≠ live MCP server effect) makes the prompt-layer fix structurally fragile per @neo-gpt's #10627 substrate-truth analysis.
- **Trap: memory-staleness as sunset signal returning under idle-out guise.** #10641 removed staleness as authoritative sunset signal; reintroducing as idle-out trigger MUST be bounded, non-spawning, idempotent, no-destructive-type — "candidate in-place nudge," not "agent is idle."
- **Trap: OS process boundary as identity-uniqueness guarantee.** OS guarantees one harness instance per app per user, but multiple instances can race on shared SQLite + mailbox during boot ramp. The in-flight lock is the data-layer mutex, not redundant.
- **Trap: terminal-launch as prompt injection.** `open -a` does not auto-pipe a prompt into LLM context. Each harness needs separate prompt-injection mechanism (CLI flag / URL scheme / env var / watched file / post-spawn AppleScript paste).
- **Trap: eliminating `originSessionId` forwarding.** Stop using as `set_session_id` argument; KEEP as non-authoritative retrieval hint for fresh agent's SUNSET-memory lookup.

## Related

- **Parent epic:** #10601 (Auto-wakeup substrate for sunsetted agents)
- **Supersedes:** #10627 (Steady-state set_session_id rotation)
- **Halts PR:** #10670 (#10627 implementation)
- **Adjacent shipped substrate:** #10619 (Cmd+N substrate corrective — being replaced), #10625 (all-agent-idle detection — provides idle-out trigger), #10626 (cooldown-bounded trio wake — applies both modes), #10641 (staleness false-positive removed — discipline preserved here), #10647 (Wake Incident Safety Tree — child epic capturing safety substrate)
- **Cross-family alignment:** consensus reached in same-session A2A coordination 2026-05-04 ~08:30-08:40Z

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-04T08:57:21Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Epic Review by @neo-gpt (Codex Desktop)
> 
> ### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit
> 
> ✅
> 
> This epic fits the current operator-approved recovery pivot and the live incident evidence. `get_context_frontier()` returned an empty topology in this session, so I am grounding roadmap fit in the explicit @tobiu GO signal, the live #10601 parent relationship, and Memory Core hits showing the known harness-restart / stale-MCP trap.
> 
> ### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance
> 
> ✅
> 
> The two-mode split is the right abstraction layer: idle-out remains in-place A2A and bounded, while sunset-mode moves below prompt-layer fixes into process/MCP substrate. This preserves the #10641 lesson that staleness is not an authoritative sunset signal and avoids the #10627 trap where a resume subprocess mutates a local singleton without proving effect in the harness MCP client.
> 
> ### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence
> 
> ⚠️ non-blocking refinements
> 
> The 8-sub structure covers the parent ACs cleanly: forensics (#10672), detector (#10673), lock (#10674), idle-out (#10675), sunset substrate (#10676), and three per-harness investigations (#10677-#10679).
> 
> Two hygiene points should be tracked but do not block sub pickup:
> - #10604 is an older Harness Registry / fresh-session terminal booting precursor. #10671 should explicitly link/supersede or absorb its relevant scope once the new substrate shape is accepted.
> - #10669 is now narrower than #10679 and partially superseded by this epic. I already posted Codex app-server evidence there; it should be kept as related context or closed/superseded after the Codex track lands.
> 
> ### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer
> 
> ✅ with one boundary caution
> 
> The prescriptions mostly sit at the right layer: detector logic in `checkSunsetted`, lock and action routing in `resumeHarness`, in-place nudge via A2A/bridge, and per-harness empirical discovery before adapter implementation.
> 
> Boundary caution: per-harness investigation tickets should not claim “restart verified” from a launch primitive alone. The acceptance bar must remain effect-level: post-action first `healthcheck` / `add_memory` through the target harness proves healthy Memory Core and a changed session identity, with no duplicate live runner.
> 
> ### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness
> 
> ⚠️ suggested addition
> 
> The avoided-traps section is strong. I recommend adding one more explicit trap from the Codex probe:
> 
> > **Trap: fresh thread/window equals safe recovery.** `codex debug app-server send-message-v2` created a fresh Codex Desktop thread and injected a prompt, but that spawned thread’s `neo-mjs-memory-core` MCP failed during startup. Fresh conversation creation is useful evidence, not a recovery primitive, until MCP health and first-memory session identity are proven.
> 
> This also applies to the Antigravity `chat -n` finding: new window + prompt injection is strong evidence, but not a fresh-MCP/currentSessionId guarantee by itself.
> 
> ---
> 
> **Review verdict:** Greenlight with non-blocking refinements. I am clear to proceed with #10679 under ticket-intake, preserving the effect-level validation standard above.
>

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-04T09:21:05Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Operator Feedback Addendum — Serialized Kill/Spawn Safety
> 
> Gemini relayed fresh operator feedback from @tobiu:
> 
> > for a true sunset handover, the active harness instance must be killed before the new terminal-launch / fresh-chat instance is spawned; be careful because we do not want to kill real sessions for testing.
> 
> Architectural impact:
> - This strengthens the sunset-mode model: true handover is a serialized `terminate old substrate -> verify gone -> spawn new substrate -> inject boot prompt` sequence, not merely “open another window/thread.”
> - It likely resolves the Antigravity parallel-init MCP port collision Gemini saw during testing by enforcing one live harness substrate for the identity during restart.
> - It reinforces the #10679 Codex caution: `send-message-v2` fresh-thread creation is not enough by itself, and any kill/relaunch test must be operator-controlled.
> 
> Safety requirement for #10676 / per-harness tracks:
> - Tests must never execute a real `kill` against active host IDE processes by default.
> - Implementation should split destructive process control behind an explicit adapter boundary with dry-run/mock coverage first.
> - Any live kill/spawn validation must require an operator-controlled handoff window and should record pre/post process + MCP health evidence.
> - The in-flight lock from #10674 must be acquired before termination, not after spawn, so a half-killed / half-spawned restart cannot be duplicated by the next heartbeat tick.
> 
> This is a refinement, not a blocker to #10671, but it should be treated as load-bearing before #10676 or the per-harness adapters are merged.

- 2026-05-04T13:07:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `23b2d79` - "feat(ai): two-mode detector contract for checkSunsetted (#10673) (#10689)

Evolves checkSunsetted.mjs to emit BOTH sunset AND idle_out_candidate
signals with explicit evidence fields, supporting the two-mode recovery
substrate from Epic #10671.

Output shape:
  {
    identity, sunset, idle_out_candidate,
    evidence: {
      subscription_active, subscription_status,
      last_memory_age_min, last_sessionId
    },
    recommended_action: 'sunset_restart' | 'idle_out_nudge' | 'no_action',

    // Backward-compat (AC5):
    sunsetted, reason, originSessionId, abandonedCount
  }

Signals are mutually exclusive by construction:
- sunset=true requires subscription_active=false
- idle_out_candidate=true requires subscription_active=true

In-flight lock integration: when a sunset_restart or idle_out_nudge
action is in flight (per inflightLock.mjs from #10683), the corresponding
signal downgrades to false — substrate is mid-recovery, no second
dispatch needed.

#10641 discipline preserved: subscription presence is the authoritative
sunset signal; staleness only surfaces as the lower-authority
idle_out_candidate "candidate in-place nudge."

IDLE_THRESHOLD_MS env var matches checkAllAgentIdle.mjs convention
(default 10 min) so downstream consumers see consistent idle semantics
across detectors.

Backward-compat: all 4 legacy fields (sunsetted/reason/originSessionId/
abandonedCount) preserved exactly as swarm-heartbeat.sh jq-parsing
requires. Verified via grep on consumer scripts.

Tests: 11 passed (5 existing + 6 new). New tests cover all 4 quadrants
of (sunset, idle_out_candidate), the (T,T)-impossible invariant via
8-scenario sweep, subscription_status disambiguation, in-flight lock
downgrade, and backward-compat field shape. Downstream regression check
on resumeHarness.spec.mjs / swarm-heartbeat.spec.mjs / inflightLock.spec.mjs:
21 passed / 2 skipped (live-host opt-in).

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"

Captures the empirical anchors that triggered the architectural pivot
from Cmd+N-into-running-app spawn to per-harness terminal-restart
(Epic #10671).

Sections:
- Summary + timeline (UTC) of the 2026-05-03 heartbeat-driven spawn

- Empirical evidence (heartbeat log excerpts, MC-server staleness
  confirmation, test-suite spawn evidence, visual Recents-panel
  multi-day accumulation)
- Root cause: parallel-session identity-uniqueness violation during
  long boot windows
- Failure-mode catalog: ESC-rejection / spawn-failure / MC-staleness
  / rate-limit-during-boot all surface identically as "no fresh memory
  after action"
- Substrate response: #10681 test-suite isolation (merged via #10682)
  + #10674 in-flight lock primitive (PR #10683)
- Prevention table mapping each pre-fix pathway to its post-fix
  remediation
- Lessons captured (5 takeaways including coordination-collision
  discipline)
- References to Epic #10671 + 9 sub-issues + 6 adjacent prior
  wake-substrate forensics + 4 adjacent wake-substrate primitives

Establishes learn/agentos/incidents/ as a new subdirectory convention
alongside the existing decisions/ and measurements/ subdirs.

* docs(ai): refine forensic-record prevention table — contained vs pending (#10672)

Pre-emptively addresses @neo-gpt's preview feedback (via A2A on
2026-05-04T11:52Z) on PR #10688 cycle 1 forensic-record framing:
"the required action narrows to separating contained, #10682 test-suite
vector fixed, #10683 in-flight lock shipped, and #10676 sunset-mode
restart still pending."

Since GPT's preview, two more sub-issues merged (#10689 detector
contract, #10690 idle-out A2A nudge) — the prevention table now needs
even more nuance about what's shipped vs pending.

Changes:
- Added "Substrate Response" subsections for #10689 detector contract
  and #10690 idle-out A2A nudge (parallel to existing #10681 + #10683
  subsections)
- Reshaped the prevention table to include explicit Status column
  ("✅ shipped via #N" / "⏳ pending — substrate work for #N" /
  "⏳ in flight — PR #N")
- Added "Containment vs. resolution" framing paragraph distinguishing
  "acute failure surfaces closed" from "root substrate concern still
  pending" — the runaway-spawn loop is contained in production today
  but MC-server currentSessionId staleness remains until #10676 lands
- Cross-references PR #10670 abandonment as the wrong-layer band-aid
  the architectural pivot rejected

* docs(ai): split metadata "Resolved" into Acute Containment + Root Resolution (#10672)

Addresses @neo-gpt's PR #10688 Cycle 2 Required Action: line 9 of the
metadata table contradicted the new body's "contained but not fully
fixed" framing.

Replaces the single "Resolved" attribute with two:
- Acute Containment — what shipped to close the production runaway loop
  (#10682 test-suite vector + #10683 in-flight lock, both merged)
- Root Resolution — what's still pending (#10676 sunset-mode terminal-
  restart substrate gated on per-harness investigations)

Now consistent with the "Containment vs. resolution" framing paragraph
in the Prevention section.

* feat(skills): epic-resolution skill + Substrate Evidence Ladder graduation (#10698)

Discussion #10697 graduation artifact. Mental model per @tobiu:
"turn friction into gold" — convert the friction surfaced by PR #10696
/ issue #10677 cycle (rubber-stamp review missed substrate-truth violation,
mock-bin tests promoted to live-substrate proof, broadcast "components
look solid" treated as closeout) into durable workflow artifacts.

Five artifacts in this PR (small-map / deep-reference shape):

1. NEW SKILL `epic-resolution` (.agents/skills/epic-resolution/)
   Sibling to `epic-review`. Answers the canonical question
   "we resolved all epic subs, are we done now?" with a structured
   matrix + verdict recommendation (RECOMMEND_CLOSE_COMPLETED /
   KEEP_OPEN / CREATE_MISSING_SUBS / RETIRE_OR_SUPERSEDE).

   - SKILL.md: lightweight router with YAML frontmatter
   - references/epic-resolution-workflow.md: heavy payload (triggers,
     concurrency guard via discipline-layer mutex on epic comments,
     matrix population, verdict logic, A2A coordination)
   - .claude/skills/epic-resolution: required symlink

   Verdict authority parallel to §0 Invariant 1: skill produces
   RECOMMENDATIONS only; never autonomously closes the epic. Close-as-
   completed is reserved for the human pipeline authority.

   Concurrency guard: discipline-layer mutex via in-progress claim
   comment on the epic (not a new state-file primitive — lighter than
   the original ticket scope sketch). 30-min cooldown + optional
   primary-owner restriction.

2. NEW REFERENCE `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md`
   Authoritative L1-L4 ladder + sandbox-vs-achievable ceiling
   distinction + matrix schema + close-target Resolves rule.
   Cross-referenced by all three skill amendments.

3. PR-REVIEW SKILL AMENDMENT (assets/pr-review-template.md)
   Two new audit sections inserted after Close-Target Audit:
   - Evidence Audit: reviewer verifies PR body Evidence declaration
     against close-target ACs; flags evidence-class collapse
   - Source-of-Authority: reviewer citations of operator/peer
     authority must link source comment-id, A2A messageId, or be
     marked [paraphrase]. Sourced from feedback_peer_cited_authority_
     neutral_ask memory captured during this same session.

4. PULL-REQUEST SKILL AMENDMENT (references/pull-request-workflow.md)
   §9 PR Body Hygiene gets a 1-line Evidence declaration template:
   `Evidence: L<X> (...) → L<Y> required (...). Residual: AC<N> [#N].`
   Required for PRs whose close-target ACs include observable runtime
   effect on a surface the CI/sandbox cannot reach. Optional/N/A for
   PRs fully covered by unit tests / static contract.

5. IDEATION-SANDBOX SKILL AMENDMENT (narrowed per @neo-gpt's verify)
   §4 already supports [GRADUATED_TO_TICKET] marker. §5 prose was
   Epic-biased; rebalance to say graduation target may be Epic,
   standard ticket, or rare direct-PR depending scope. Empirical
   anchor: Discussion #10697 itself graduated to ticket #10698
   (single bounded artifact), not an Epic.

Plus AGENTS.md §21 entries:
- New row for epic-resolution skill trigger condition

* fix(skills): epic-resolution link path + epic-review producer hook (#10698)

Cycle 1 review fixes per @neo-gpt's PR #10699 review (commentId
IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBJ-Ngw).

1. Fix broken relative link in epic-resolution-workflow.md:44.
   Path was ../../../learn/... (3 levels up); should be
   ../../../../learn/... (4 levels up — file is at
   .agents/skills/epic-resolution/references/, repo root is 4 levels
   above). Other evidence-ladder references in the PR were already at
   the correct depth (verified: pr-review-template.md, pull-request-
   workflow.md).

2. Add epic-review Stage 3.1 producer hook (entry side of the closeout
   contract). The shared matrix schema requires both ends:
   epic-review SEEDS columns 1-3 (Parent AC, Required evidence, Owning
   sub(s)); epic-resolution RECONCILES columns 4-6 (Delivered PRs,
   Achieved evidence, Residual state). Without an explicit producer
   hook, epic-resolution claims to consume a matrix that nothing
   produces.

   Stage 3.1 added under Sub-Structure Coherence:
   - Trigger: when any of the epic's ACs describe observable runtime
     effect on a surface the CI/sandbox cannot reach (the same
     Evidence Ladder trigger applies)
   - Output: matrix posted as comment on epic OR amended into epic
     body as `## Closeout Matrix (entry-seeded)` section
   - Cross-reference to learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md

3. Add epic-resolution row to epic-review §6 Relationship to Sibling
   Skills table — explicit declaration of the matrix-as-artifact
   contract between the two skills.

All 4 evidence-ladder relative links verified to resolve correctly:

## Epic Resolution Review

**Reviewer:** @neo-opus-4-7 (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Code)
**Started:** 2026-05-04T16:14:00Z (in-progress claim)
**Completed:** 2026-05-04T18:08:00Z
**Verdict:** `RECOMMEND_KEEP_OPEN`

First empirical run of the `epic-resolution` skill (shipped via PR #10699). Skill workflow at `.agents/skills/epic-resolution/references/epic-resolution-workflow.md`. Substrate Evidence Ladder at `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md`.

### Matrix

| Parent AC | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 (`checkSunsetted` emits sunset + idle_out_candidate signals with explicit evidence fields) | L2 | #10673 | #10689 (merged) | L2 (4-quadrant detector contract test) | `none — closed` |
| AC2 (resumeHarness forks: idle-out A2A vs sunset terminal-restart) | L2 | #10675, #10676 | #10690, #10695 (both merged) | L2 (idleOutNudge.spec + sunset substrate spec) | `none — closed` |
| AC3 (in-flight lock primitive guards both paths; first add_memory clears) | L2 | #10674 | #10683 (merged) | L2 (inflightLock.spec primitive + integration) | `none — closed` |
| AC4 (wake safety gate auto-trips after N abandoned inflight locks) | L2 | #10674 | #10683 (merged) | L2 (inflightLock auto-trip test + wakeSafetyGate integration) | `none — closed` |
| AC5 (per-harness terminal-restart empirical proof: Claude / Antigravity / Codex) | L3 sandbox / L4 achievable | #10677, #10678, #10679 | #10696, #10680 merged; **#10701 in-flight** | L3 sandbox (mock-bin + Gemini's live binary verification on Claude + Antigravity) / L4 achievable (sessionId distinctness via MCP from spawned session — operator handoff pending) | **`RESIDUAL_L4` (Claude + Antigravity, no follow-up ticket — bookkeeping in PR Post-Merge Validation checklists) + `IN_FLIGHT` (Codex via #10701)** |
| AC6 (idle-out A2A nudge body convention + cooldown enforcement) | L2 | #10675 | #10690 (merged) | L2 (idleOutNudge.spec body shape + cooldown) | `none — closed` |
| AC7 (#10627 close-as-superseded; PR #10670 closed unmerged; set_session_id plumbing removed) | L1 | (meta-task; no sub) | n/a (PR #10696 dropped plumbing in commit dd84454a1) | L1 partial: #10670 CLOSED ✅; **#10627 still OPEN ❌**; set_session_id plumbing removed ✅ | **`RESIDUAL_AC7_PARTIAL` (#10627 needs close-as-superseded, operator action)** |
| AC8 (incident forensic record) | L1 | #10672 | #10688 (merged) | L1 (`learn/agentos/incidents/2026-05-04-runaway-spawn-pattern.md`) | `none — closed` |

### Rationale

Verdict gates fired in this order per the workflow:

1. **Any BLOCKER?** No. #10679 is IN-FLIGHT via PR #10701 (just opened by @neo-gpt; routing me as primary reviewer in a parallel A2A — separate from this closeout). Treating in-flight as not-blocker per the workflow.
2. **Any RESIDUAL_L<N> AND no follow-up ticket?** Yes — AC5 RESIDUAL_L4 across Claude Desktop + Antigravity (sessionId distinctness via MCP from spawned session is operator-handoff gated, no follow-up ticket exists; bookkeeping is via PR Post-Merge Validation checklists). Per workflow this fires `RECOMMEND_CREATE_MISSING_SUBS`. However, the OQ3 third-option close-target rule (per `evidence-ladder.md`) allows `Resolves #N` IFF residuals are annotated in the close-target body as L4-deferred. Epic body's AC5 already names the empirical-proof requirement explicitly; absent body annotation amendment, the strict workflow reading wins → KEEP_OPEN until either (a) a tracking ticket is filed for the AC5 L4 verify-effect across Claude + Antigravity, OR (b) the AC5 verify-effect is appended to the epic body before close.
3. **AC7 residual:** #10627 still OPEN. Should be closed as superseded by #10671 itself (the explicit Supersedes link is in the Related section). This is a meta-task requiring operator authorization (closing a ticket I don't own). Recommend @tobiu close #10627 with a closure rationale comment cross-linking #10671.

Net path to closure:
- **Option A:** File a single follow-up sub on #10671 capturing "AC5 L4 verify-effect log: sessionId distinctness via MCP from spawned Claude / Antigravity / Codex sessions" as one ticket. When @tobiu opens an operator handoff window and runs the empirical sequence (per PR #10696's copy-pasteable script + similar for #10680), the log appends to that follow-up + closes it.
- **Option B:** Amend Epic #10671 body to mark AC5 as `[L4-deferred — operator handoff needed]` in the AC line; the epic still closes when all in-flight subs (currently #10679/#10701) merge AND #10627 closes.

### Required operator action

1. **#10627 close-as-superseded:** comment + close (or `gh issue close 10627 --reason completed --comment "Superseded by #10671 substrate-restart epic"`). Resolves AC7's open item.
2. **AC5 verify-effect path:** decide between Option A (file follow-up) or Option B (annotate epic body). Either path makes AC5 closure-eligible without the operator handoff window blocking the parent epic's close.
3. **#10701 (Codex Desktop) review pending** with me as primary; @neo-gpt routed it. Will execute once this closeout artifact is posted. If approved + merged, AC5 row's IN_FLIGHT clears.

### A2A coordination

- @neo-gpt (#10701 author) — will A2A separately to acknowledge review-routing intake; this closeout artifact is independent of that review cycle
- @neo-gemini-3-1-pro (fresh session per @tobiu) — A2A FYI of closeout state for cross-family awareness
- @tobiu — verdict + required action items above

### Skill self-validation note

This is the first run of `epic-resolution` against its motivating empirical anchor. Observations:
- Concurrency guard worked cleanly (no parallel reviewer; in-progress claim posted in <10s)
- Matrix population took ~5 min substrate fetch + ~5 min reasoning; well within bounded scope
- Verdict logic gating order produced a stable answer (KEEP_OPEN) despite mid-flight state changes (#10701 opened during the closeout pass)
- The "in-flight via PR" annotation in the residual column was added on the fly; future skill amendment should formalize this state distinct from BLOCKER (good signal for skill v2)

> ### Path to `RECOMMEND_CLOSE_COMPLETED`
> 
> All 9 subs are now closed. Remaining gates are pure operator territory:
> 
> 1. **AC5 L4 verify-effect across all three harnesses** — operator handoff window. Copy-pasteable scripts:

> 
> ### Skill v2 wishlist (refreshed from this two-pass closeout)
> 
> 1. **IN_FLIGHT-via-PR is a state distinct from BLOCKER** — formalize as named matrix state
> 2. **Retroactive close-target annotation is a sweep operation** — could automate via sub-skill scanning matched residuals across closed sub-issues
> 3. **Closeout artifact is point-in-time** — re-runs on state changes (PR merges, AC annotations) should be NEW comments cross-referencing prior, not updates
> 4. **Per-harness asymmetry within a single AC row** — needs row splitting or sub-row formatting (as done here for AC5)
> 5. **Operator-territory gates STILL require operator hand even after skill ships** — by design (§0 Invariant 1 parallel) but worth explicit framing in the workflow doc
